title: cyperus oblongus subsp. nervosus
intro: Cyperus oblongus subsp. nervosus, also known as the Nardoo or Nardoo fern, is a subspecies of the Cyperus oblongus plant, which is native to Australia and is a type of sedge. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Cyperus oblongus subsp. nervosus include its potential to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a diuretic to help with urinary issues. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical applications, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
